a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Relative poverty: Share of people below 50% of median income, 2023
Relative poverty is measured in terms of a poverty line that rises and falls over time with average incomes – in
this case set at 50% of median income.
Data source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2024)

Note: Depending on the country and year, the data relates to income measured after taxes and benefits, or to consumption,
per capita
.
